shapes.note: sparse-MLA prefill; reference dispatches to TileLang on this ROCm build

baseline_kernel: sglang TileLang sparse-MLA kernel (_try_sglang_tilelang_sparse_mla; sgl_kernel.flash_mla has no ROCm sparse_prefill_fwd op)

bottleneck.evidence: result.json per_shape: reference 8457/17187/34614us at MFU 0.026/0.026/0.026 -- a slow monolithic compiled TileLang kernel with NO launch-config knob to override; candidate 6516/13154/26450us at MFU 0.034 (compute-bound, AI 1289/1547/1719)

approaches.summary: Independent torch gather+attention reimplementation of sparse-MLA, tuned to PASS the calc_diff gate by raising ONLY the QK matmul to fp32 to match the reference's fp32 logits
approaches.why: the TileLang reference is slow and knob-less so the only lever is a faster reimpl that passes the gate; fp32 QK gives calc_diff 2.88e-6 (< 5e-6, ~1.7x margin) and ~1.3x faster

approaches.summary: Same torch reimpl but with the default bf16 QK matmul
approaches.why: bf16 QK matmul rounds the logits -> calc_diff 6.5e-6 which FAILS the 5e-6 gate; correctness here requires MORE numerical precision than the naive path, not less

approaches.summary: Override a launch-config knob like the MoE targets
approaches.why: the TileLang sparse-MLA reference exposes no config knob to override, so the MoE-style bit-exact lever is not available

lesson: When the reference is a slow monolithic compiled kernel with no config knob, correctness is defined by the official gate (calc_diff<=5e-6), NOT by bit-exactness. A faster independent reimplementation that passes the gate is legitimate -- and passing may require being MORE precise than the naive path (fp32 QK matmul to match the reference's fp32 logits), not less.
transfers_to: attention/MLA prefill tasks whose reference is a slow compiled/TileLang kernel with no tunable launch config
cases where a torch reimpl is fast but the naive dtype fails the diff gate
caveats: Not bit-exact (calc_diff 2.88e-6) -- margin to the 5e-6 gate must be re-verified if the gate tightens
Depends on the TileLang path remaining the (slow) reference on this ROCm build
